data.content_ref: inline:{"analogy":{"adopted_commitment_refs":[],"disanalogies":["Two-phase-commit coordinators are typically replicated for fault tolerance; the harness is singleton","Database shadow state is usually for performance; this shadow state is for model conditioning","Refinement proofs assume static specifications; model behavior is inherently stochastic"],"overturn_conditions":["The scheduler's school conditioning is proven to be a no-op at the byte level of the wire contract","The harness already includes prompt assembly in its formal replay state (not just post-adapter)","A deterministic equivalence is proven between pre- and post-adapter representations"],"shared_structure":["A canonical coordinator (manifest/harness) claims to control decisions","An auxiliary process (scheduler/prompt-assembler) pre-conditions inputs with decision-relevant information","The downstream verifier (adapter/commit protocol) checks formal properties of already-conditioned inputs","The shadow decision is invisible to replay because it lives in transient pre-verification state","Post-hoc analysis cannot distinguish 'correctly followed policy' from 'happened to match policy'"],"source_memory_refs":["distributed-systems-literature: two-phase-commit-with-coordinator-failure","formal-methods: shadow-state-in-refinement-proofs"],"transfer_claims":["The 'coordinator bypass' pattern from distributed systems applies: any pre-verification conditioning of inputs that affects downstream behavior without being in the formal commit log creates an unrecoverable consistency gap","The refinement proof technique of 'lifting shadow state to visible state' applies: the school conditioning must be made an explicit part of the wire contract and committed before adapter verification","The migration strategy of 'dual-write then cutover' applies: run both implicit and explicit routing in parallel, compare outcomes, then eliminate implicit path"]},"claim":"The Jolt architecture suffers from a delegation boundary violation where school-conditioned prompt assembly in src/deepreason/scheduler/scheduler.py:410-520 and src/deepreason/rules/conj.py:43-84 creates an implicit route-binding through prose conditioning, circumventing the manifest's immutable role-route freeze and enabling model-side policy drift that the harness cannot detect or replay.","counterconditions":[{"case":"The scheduler's school conditioning is proven to have zero effect on actual model dispatch (adapter ignores it, routes purely by manifest)","eval":"observation"},{"case":"All school-conditioned packs are byte-for-byte identical regardless of school identity","eval":"observation"},{"case":"The harness already commits full prompt assembly state to the formal ledger","eval":"observation"},{"case":"Route leases in adapter.py:300-430 include school identity in the wire contract derivation","eval":"observation"}],"definitions":[],"derivation":[],"formalization_refs":[],"mechanism":"The scheduler iterates schools as prompt/provenance conditioning (not as distinct configured routes), and conj deterministically renders a pack with school conditioning but no scratch attention pack. This creates a two-stage pipeline where: (1) the manifest freezes role routes at commit time (run_manifest.py:412-500), (2) the scheduler injects school identity into prompt context without binding to a route lease, (3) the adapter verifies route leases (adapter.py:300-430) but receives already-conditioned packs where the school identity has been merged into prompt prose, making lease verification blind to the actual routing decision. The harness replays formal state correctly (harness.py:69-116) but cannot replay the implicit school-to-route binding because it exists only in transient prompt assembly state, not in committed events. This violates C4 (one bounded model responsibility per call) by allowing the scheduler to make routing-equivalent decisions, C5 (prose cannot alter routes) by embedding route information in prose, and C9 (formal epistemic status canonical) by creating a shadow routing layer outside the formal ledger.","premises":[],"schema":"deepreason-reasoning-envelope-v1","scope":{"covers":[],"excludes":[]},"simulation_refs":[],"uncertainties":[]}
